title: thalictrum cultratum subsp. platycarpum
intro: Thalictrum cultratum subsp. platycarpum, commonly known as meadow rue, is a perennial plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of this plant include relieving symptoms of colds and flu, as well as reducing inflammation and improving digestion. Herbal preparations of meadow rue can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it can also be used in salads or as a garnish for its delicate flowers and leaves.
